summary:
The S&P 500 snapped a five-week losing streak last week with a 3.4% gain, closing near 6,611, as markets stabilized after weeks of Middle East-driven selling.
The Iran conflict — now in its 39th day — remains the dominant macro force, with Trump threatening to strike Iranian infrastructure and Kharg Island already hit, keeping oil prices elevated.
tweet: Iran conflict and Fed hold fears drove UBS to cut its S&P 500 year-end target to 7,500 — stocks just snapped a 5-week losing streak but the ceiling is getting lower. --- SUMMARY: The S&P 500 snapped a five-week losing...
